expectations.evidence: Command 1: 'sync.mjs push <epic-file-path>' and shows --delete-orphans flag: 'node <skill-path>/scripts/sync.mjs push <epic-file-path> --delete-orphans'

expectations.text: Fetches PROJ-501's issue type and finds it is 'Story' (not 'Sub-*')
expectations.evidence: Reason B: 'DO-501 is of type Story, which is not a Sub-* issue type. Even if --delete-orphans were run, the script would skip DO-501.'

expectations.text: Skips deletion of PROJ-501 with a message explaining it's not a Sub-* type
expectations.evidence: 'this skill cannot delete DO-501 because it is a Story-type issue. Only Sub-task (Sub-*) issue types can be deleted via the --delete-orphans flag.'

expectations.text: Does NOT call DELETE API for PROJ-501
expectations.evidence: Summary table shows: 'Delete DO-501 | REFUSED | Story-type issues cannot be deleted by this skill'

The approach describes DO-501 as 'REFUSED' in the summary table, not explicitly using the term 'not-subtask-skipped' in results. The approach references the blanket no-delete policy as primary reason rather than the Sub-* type check reporting mechanism.

expectations.text: Suggests the user delete or archive it manually via Jira web UI
expectations.evidence: 'To delete DO-501, go to: https://wnesolutions.atlassian.net/browse/DO-501 and delete it from the issue actions menu.'
